title: [rtl] Do not register instruction fetch errors
body:
Registering instruction fetch errors causes problem in the case of seeing
a PMP execution access block. ID/EX stage learns about
the instruction that it executed should be blocked a
cycle late. This commit fixes that.

Found this one while working on fixing https://github.com/lowRISC/ibex/issues/1720. However it does not fully solves the issue because we are still seeing some mismatches because of some self modifying code problems.

Signed-off-by: Canberk Topal <ctopal@lowrisc.org>

repo.name: ibex
repo.description: Ibex is a small 32 bit RISC-V CPU core, previously known as zero-riscy.

commits.message:
[rtl] Flush controller in PMP CSR write ops

As Greg pointed out:

When we have an instruction in ID/EX that writes a PMP register that
update gets written to the CSR the same cycle the next instruction
moves from IF to ID/EX with it's PMP check done with the old value.

The solution is to flush the pipeline when we get a PMP CSR write.

Signed-off-by: Canberk Topal <ctopal@lowrisc.org>

resolved_issues.title: [dv] Exception divergence using MML PMP mode
resolved_issues.body:
<!--
Thank you for your interest in Ibex. We are continuously improving Ibex, and your bug report will help us with that. To help us understand and reproduce your problem as quickly as possible we have prepared a little template below.

No text in blocks starting with < !-- will be shown in the final bug report.

You can also copy/paste images into the bug report, and you can attach files to it by simply dragging them into the issue window.
-->
I'm seeing a divergence between Spike and Ibex when in MML mode and setting region 0 to read only. Spike throws a trap and Ibex zeroes out a destination register. I'm not sure if this is an issue with Spike, Ibex or with the UVM comparison.

## Observed Behavior
<!--
Please describe the problem you saw. Include waveforms (in VCD or Verilator/GtkWave FST format only please) and log files if possible.
-->
Cosimulation result:
```
name:            riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test
seed:            14822
binary:          out/run/riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test.14822/test.o
uvm_log:         out/run/riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test.14822/rtl.log
rtl_trace:       out/run/riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test.14822/trace_core_00000000.log
rtl_trace_csv:   out/run/riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test.14822/trace_core_00000000.log.csv
iss_trace:       
iss_trace_csv:   
en_cosim:        True
cosim_trace:     
cosim_trace_csv: 
comparison_log:  
passed:          False
failure_message: |-
  UVM_FATAL /home/mvdmaas/ibex/dv/uvm/core_ibex/common/ibex_cosim_agent/ibex_cosim_scoreboard.sv(131) @ 7304: uvm_test_top.env.cosim_agent.scoreboard [ibex_cosim_agent_pkg::ibex_cosim_scoreboard.run_cosim_rvfi] Cosim mismatch Synchronous trap was expected at ISS PC: 80001900 but DUT didn't report one at PC 80000118
  [FAILED]: sim error seen
```

Ibex RVFI trace:

```
core   0:           tval 0x80000118
```

## Expected Behavior
<!-- Please describe the behavior you expected to see instead. -->
Not sure if it's a bug in Spike, Ibex or the cosimulation environment

## Steps to reproduce the issue
<!--
Please help us to reproduce the issue you're seeing by including a detailed description of what you did to get to the problem.

For example:
* What command did you run? (Also include the output of the command.)
* What parameters/defines did you set?
* Did you make modifications to the source code?
-->
Using the following commands from the root of the ibex repo:
```console
cd dv/uvm/core_ibex
make -j7 --keep-going IBEX_CONFIG=opentitan ITERATIONS=1 SEED=14822 TEST=riscv_epmp_mml_read_only_test WAVES=0 COV=0
```

## My Environment

<!--
Let us know how you're using Ibex. This helps us to better understand your problem and find a solution more quickly.
-->

**EDA tool and version:**
<!--
If you are using an EDA tool to work with Ibex let us know which tool and which version you're using.
For example, "Xilinx Vivado 2020.1" or "Synopsys VCS 2020.03-SP1"
-->
Using Spike version ibex_cosim on commit: 9af9730baf7b956c3072c1b436d867aca5ef8f4c

**Operating system:**
<!--
Please let us know the operating system/Linux distribution you're using.
For example "Ubuntu Linux 18.04" or "CentOS 7.3".
-->
Ubuntu Linux 20.04

**Version of the Ibex source code:**
<!--
Which version of the Ibex source code are you using?
You can call `git rev-parse HEAD` in your Ibex source directory to get this information.
Please also let us know if you have made changes to the code.
-->
Currently running inside this PR: https://github.com/lowRISC/ibex/pull/1695

tb_script:
#!/bin/bash
cd /home/ibex
set -uo pipefail
set +e

TEST_NAME="pmp_ifetch_flush"
TB_DIR="/home/ibex/.tb_${TEST_NAME}"
SIM_LOG="/home/ibex/${TEST_NAME}_sim.log"
SW_LOG="/home/ibex/${TEST_NAME}_sw_build.log"
RUNTIME_LOG="/home/ibex/ibex_simple_system.log"
SIM_BIN="/home/ibex/build/lowrisc_ibex_ibex_simple_system_0/sim-verilator/Vibex_simple_system"

finish_test() {
  local status="$1"
  local exit_code="$2"

  echo "HWE_BENCH_RESULTS_START"
  echo "TEST: ${TEST_NAME} ... ${status}"
  echo "HWE_BENCH_RESULTS_END"
  exit "${exit_code}"
}

rm -rf "${TB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${TB_DIR}"

cat > "${TB_DIR}/Makefile" <<'EOF'
PROGRAM = pmp_ifetch_flush
PROGRAM_DIR := $(shell dirname $(realpath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
EXTRA_SRCS := $(PROGRAM_DIR)/test_main.S
include /home/ibex/examples/sw/simple_system/common/common.mk
EOF

cat > "${TB_DIR}/pmp_ifetch_flush.c" <<'EOF'
/* Intentionally empty: main is implemented in pmp_ifetch_flush.S. */
EOF

cat > "${TB_DIR}/test_main.S" <<'EOF'
// Copyright lowRISC contributors.
// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

  .section .text
  .globl main
  .option norvc

main:
  la   a0, blocked_insn
  srli a0, a0, 2
  csrw pmpaddr0, a0
  li   a0, 0x90
  csrw pmpcfg0, a0

blocked_insn:
  j    after_blocked

after_blocked:
  la   a0, no_trap_msg
  call puts
  call sim_halt
1:
  j    1b

  .section .rodata
no_trap_msg:
  .asciz "NO_TRAP\n"
EOF

make -C "${TB_DIR}" CC=riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc ARCH=rv32im > "${SW_LOG}" 2>&1
sw_rc=$?
cat "${SW_LOG}"
if [ ${sw_rc} -ne 0 ]; then
  finish_test "FAIL" "${sw_rc}"
fi

python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

path = Path("/home/ibex/examples/simple_system/ibex_simple_system.cc")
text = path.read_text()
old = """std::string SimpleSystem::GetIsaString() const {
  const Vibex_simple_system &top = _top;
  assert(top.ibex_simple_system);

  std::string base = top.ibex_simple_system->RV32E ? "rv32e" : "rv32i";

  std::string extensions;
  if (top.ibex_simple_system->RV32M)
    extensions += "m";

  extensions += "c";

  switch (top.ibex_simple_system->RV32B) {
    case 0:  // RV32BNone
      break;

    case 1:  // RV32BBalanced
      extensions += "_Zba_Zbb_Zbs_XZbf_XZbt";
      break;

    case 2:  // RV32BOTEarlGrey
      extensions += "_Zba_Zbb_Zbc_Zbs_XZbf_XZbp_XZbr_XZbt";
      break;

    case 3:  // RV32BFull
      extensions += "_Zba_Zbb_Zbc_Zbs_XZbe_XZbf_XZbp_XZbr_XZbt";
      break;
  }

  return base + extensions;
}
"""
new = """std::string SimpleSystem::GetIsaString() const {
  return "rv32imc";
}
"""

if new not in text:
    if old not in text:
        raise SystemExit("Failed to apply simple_system compatibility patch")
    path.write_text(text.replace(old, new))
PY
patch_rc=$?
if [ ${patch_rc} -ne 0 ]; then
  finish_test "FAIL" "${patch_rc}"
fi

rm -f "${SIM_LOG}" "${RUNTIME_LOG}" /home/ibex/ibex_simple_system_pcount.csv /home/ibex/trace_core_00000000.log
rm -rf /home/ibex/build/lowrisc_ibex_ibex_simple_system_0

fusesoc --cores-root=. run --target=sim --tool=verilator --setup --build \
  lowrisc:ibex:ibex_simple_system \
  --RV32E=0 --RV32M=ibex_pkg::RV32MFast --PMPEnable=1 > "${SIM_LOG}" 2>&1
build_rc=$?
if [ ${build_rc} -ne 0 ]; then
  cat "${SIM_LOG}"
  finish_test "FAIL" "${build_rc}"
fi

"${SIM_BIN}" --meminit=ram,"${TB_DIR}/pmp_ifetch_flush.elf" >> "${SIM_LOG}" 2>&1
run_rc=$?
cat "${SIM_LOG}"
if [ -f "${RUNTIME_LOG}" ]; then
  echo "--- ${RUNTIME_LOG} ---"
  cat "${RUNTIME_LOG}"
fi

if [ ${run_rc} -eq 0 ] && \
   [ -f "${RUNTIME_LOG}" ] && \
   grep -q "EXCEPTION!!!" "${RUNTIME_LOG}" && \
   grep -q "MCAUSE: 0x00000001" "${RUNTIME_LOG}" && \
   ! grep -q "NO_TRAP" "${RUNTIME_LOG}"; then
  finish_test "PASS" 0
fi

finish_test "FAIL" 1
prepare_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd /home/ibex

git checkout -f 23806e2ad7d0d6fab429ae227d9a45cbd42f0fb3
git submodule update --init --recursive

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update
apt-get install -y \
  git \
  make \
  gcc \
  g++ \
  pkg-config \
  libelf-dev \
  srecord \
  python3-pip \
  python3-setuptools \
  python3-wheel \
  python3-yaml \
  python3-dev \
  flex \
  bison \
  device-tree-compiler \
  verilator \
  gcc-riscv64-unknown-elf \
  binutils-riscv64-unknown-elf

python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
python3 -m pip install -r python-requirements.txt

cat > /etc/profile.d/ibex-path.sh <<'EOF'
export PATH=/opt/micromamba/envs/ibex/bin:$PATH
EOF
chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/ibex-path.sh
export PATH=/opt/micromamba/envs/ibex/bin:$PATH

git rev-parse HEAD | tee /tmp/ibex_baseline_commit.txt
problem_statement: Ibex can apply a Physical Memory Protection (PMP) update one instruction late. When software writes a PMP address or configuration CSR so that the next instruction address becomes non-executable, the core may still fetch that next instruction and let it execute instead of faulting immediately. The expected behavior is that the updated PMP permissions take effect for the very next instruction fetch: if the next program counter is now blocked for execution, that instruction must not execute and the core must raise an instruction access fault at that program counter. This bug affects PMP-based instruction-fetch permission checking, and it is triggered by a PMP CSR write immediately followed by a fetch from the newly blocked address.
